key_points: The narrative explores psychological realism through Hari Singh’s moral struggle
First-person narration deepens emotional and ethical engagement
Anil represents humanism and reformative justice
The story rejects fear-based punishment and promotes trust-based reform
Education symbolises empowerment, dignity, and long-term change
Silence and understatement strengthen the moral impact of the ending
characters: Hari Singh – a morally conflicted narrator torn between crime and conscience
Anil – a symbolic figure of compassion, patience, and human goodness
themes: Trust as a moral risk and strength
symbols: Rain – guilt, confusion, and moral cleansing
Wet currency note – forgiveness without humiliation
exam_tips: Use terms like reformative justice and humanism in answers
Analyse narration technique for higher marks
Connect the story’s message to contemporary society
